controls.control_id: Art. 6
controls.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Rules for determining which AI systems are classified as high-risk.

controls.control_id: Art. 9
controls.title: Risk management system
controls.description: Providers of high-risk AI must implement a risk management system covering all reasonably foreseeable risks.

controls.control_id: Art. 10
controls.title: Data and data governance
controls.description: Training, validation and testing data sets shall meet quality criteria including relevance, representativeness, and freedom from errors.

controls.control_id: Art. 11
controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.description: Technical documentation shall be drawn up before placing on the market and kept up-to-date.

controls.control_id: Art. 12
controls.title: Record-keeping
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall allow automatic recording of events (logs).

controls.control_id: Art. 13
controls.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed to ensure sufficient transparency for deployers.

controls.control_id: Art. 14
controls.title: Human oversight
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed to allow effective oversight by natural persons.

controls.control_id: Art. 15
controls.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity.

controls.control_id: Art. 16
controls.title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Providers shall ensure compliance with requirements, establish quality management, and maintain documentation.

controls.control_id: Art. 17
controls.title: Quality management system
controls.description: Providers shall put a quality management system in place ensuring compliance with the regulation.

controls.control_id: Art. 25
controls.title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
controls.description: Distributors, importers, deployers, and other third parties have defined responsibilities.

controls.control_id: Art. 26
controls.title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Deployers shall use AI systems in accordance with instructions, ensure human oversight, and monitor operation.

controls.control_id: Art. 27
controls.title: Fundamental rights impact assessments
controls.description: Deployers of high-risk AI shall carry out an assessment of impact on fundamental rights before use.

controls.control_id: Art. 50
controls.title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
controls.description: AI systems that interact with people, generate deepfakes, or produce synthetic content must disclose this.

controls.control_id: Art. 51
controls.title: Classification of GPAI models as systemic risk
controls.description: GPAI models with systemic risk are classified based on high-impact capabilities or cumulative compute thresholds.

controls.control_id: Art. 53
controls.title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models
controls.description: All GPAI providers must prepare technical documentation, provide information to downstream providers, comply with copyright, and publish training data summaries.

controls.control_id: Art. 54
controls.title: Authorised representatives of GPAI model providers
controls.description: GPAI providers outside the EU must appoint an authorised representative.

controls.control_id: Art. 55
controls.title: Obligations of GPAI models with systemic risk
controls.description: Providers of GPAI with systemic risk must conduct model evaluations, assess and mitigate systemic risks, track incidents, and ensure adequate cybersecurity.

controls.control_id: Art. 56
controls.title: Codes of practice
controls.description: The AI Office shall encourage development of codes of practice for GPAI obligations.

controls.control_id: Art. 60
controls.title: AI regulatory sandboxes
controls.description: National authorities may establish regulatory sandboxes for development and testing of innovative AI.

controls.control_id: Art. 62
controls.title: Reporting of serious incidents
controls.description: Providers and deployers shall report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities.

controls.control_id: Art. 71
controls.title: Post-market monitoring by providers
controls.description: Providers shall establish a post-market monitoring system proportionate to the AI system.

controls.control_id: Art. 72
controls.title: Reporting of serious incidents
controls.description: Detailed requirements for reporting serious incidents involving AI systems.

controls.control_id: Annex III
controls.title: High-risk AI systems
controls.description: List of areas where AI systems are classified as high-risk (biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, justice).

controls.control_id: Annex IV
controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.description: Detailed requirements for technical documentation of high-risk AI systems.

controls.control_id: Goal hijack scenarios identified and mitigated in risk management system
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Agent goal hijack included in Art. 9 risk assessment for every agentic deployment

controls.control_id: Meaningful human oversight over high-risk AI system outputs
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Agents whose goals can be hijacked and execute autonomously are an Art. 14 failure � human confirmation required before goal-changing actions

controls.control_id: Technical resilience against adversarial input manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Input filtering, goal-state verification, and injection detection are Art. 15 technical requirements

controls.control_id: Tool misuse risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: All agent tool integrations assessed in Art. 9 risk management � reversibility classification documented

controls.control_id: Human oversight over high-risk AI actions
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Irreversible tool invocations require human confirmation � Art. 14 binding requirement

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures preventing tool misuse
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Per-tool permission manifests and parameter validation are Art. 15 technical requirements

controls.control_id: Credential exposure risk identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Agent credential lifecycle in Art. 9 risk management � NHI inventory, scope controls, rotation documented

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting against credential theft
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Short-lived credentials, JIT access, PKI-backed identities are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Documented procedures for credential incident response
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Agent credential incident response in quality management system � rotation, containment, lateral movement assessment

controls.control_id: Supply chain risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: All agent components in Art. 9 risk management � dynamic runtime components explicitly in scope

controls.control_id: Quality management includes supply chain controls
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Documented supply chain security procedures � component verification, change management

controls.control_id: Providers document obligations; deployers verify
controls.title: Art. 25 � Value chain responsibilities
controls.description: Agent tool and MCP server supply chain obligations distributed along value chain

controls.control_id: Code execution risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Agent code execution capability documented in Art. 9 risk management � sandbox status, permitted operations

controls.control_id: Technical robustness against adversarial code execution
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Sandboxing, input filtering, static analysis are Art. 15 requirements for agents with code execution

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering code execution incidents
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Code execution anomaly response in quality management system

controls.control_id: Data governance applies to all data influencing AI behaviour � including agent memory
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Agent memory governance � classification, access controls, integrity validation, retention � is an Art. 10 requirement

controls.control_id: Technical resilience against adversarial memory manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Memory integrity monitoring and access controls are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring for memory integrity
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Memory anomaly detection in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: A2A communication risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Inter-agent channels in Art. 9 risk assessment � authentication, encryption, schema validation status

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting all system components
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Authenticated, encrypted A2A communication is an Art. 15 requirement for high-risk agentic systems

controls.control_id: Documentation of A2A security controls
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: A2A authentication and encryption documented in quality management system

controls.control_id: Cascade risks identified and mitigated � blast radius defined
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Cascade scenarios in Art. 9 risk management � maximum affected systems, circuit breaker thresholds

controls.control_id: Human oversight over high-risk AI � ability to pause and stop
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Circuit breakers and kill switches are Art. 14 human oversight mechanisms

controls.control_id: Technical resilience against cascading failures
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Circuit breakers, fail-safe defaults, and cascade containment architecture are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Users informed of capabilities, limitations, and AI nature
controls.title: Art. 13 � Transparency
controls.description: Agents must clearly communicate their AI nature and advisory limitations

controls.control_id: Effective human oversight over high-risk AI
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Humans must be able to override agent recommendations � trust exploitation undermines Art. 14 effectiveness

controls.control_id: Chatbots and AI-generated content must disclose AI nature
controls.title: Art. 50 � Transparency for certain AI systems
controls.description: All agent-user interactions require AI disclosure � universal obligation

controls.control_id: Rogue agent scenarios identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Rogue agent risk in Art. 9 risk management � detection capability, blast radius, response documented

controls.control_id: Human oversight mechanisms effective against rogue behaviour
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Kill switch and behavioural monitoring are Art. 14 human oversight requirements for agentic systems

controls.control_id: Technical resilience against rogue agent behaviour
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Behavioural baselines, anomaly detection, and automated suspension are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering rogue agent detection
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Behavioural monitoring in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: Training data must be relevant, representative, and subject to appropriate privacy measures
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Data governance controls preventing sensitive data ingestion and memorisation are Art. 10 requirements

controls.control_id: High-risk AI must be resilient and implement cybersecurity measures
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Output scanning, DLP, and access controls on RAG retrieval are Art. 15 technical requirements

controls.control_id: GPAI providers must maintain technical documentation including training data governance
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Data governance for training data � sources, quality, privacy measures � is a GPAI documentation obligation; Data governance for GPAI training data is a binding documentation obligation from Aug 2025

controls.control_id: Credential exposure must be identified as a foreseeable risk and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Agent credential lifecycle included in Art. 9 risk management system

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting against credential theft and misuse
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Short-lived credentials, NHI inventory, and access controls are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Documented procedures for credential management and incident response
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Agent identity governance procedures documented in quality management system

controls.control_id: Shadow AI usage is a foreseeable risk requiring mitigation in the risk management system
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Ungoverned AI tool usage mapped and treated in Art. 9 risk assessment

controls.control_id: Organisations deploying AI are responsible for AI used within their operations
controls.title: Art. 25 � Value chain responsibilities
controls.description: Shadow AI use by employees does not exempt the deployer from AI Act obligations triggered by that use

controls.control_id: Deployers must use AI systems as instructed and ensure appropriate oversight
controls.title: Art. 29 � Deployer obligations
controls.description: Deployers have affirmative obligations to prevent and detect shadow AI usage

controls.control_id: Training data must be subject to governance practices � relevant, representative, free of errors
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Data quality controls and provenance requirements preventing poisoning are binding Art. 10 obligations

controls.control_id: High-risk AI must be resilient to attempts to alter performance through data manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Technical robustness against poisoning is a binding Art. 15 requirement with conformity assessment evidence; Technical robustness against poisoning is a binding Art. 15 requirement

controls.control_id: Systemic risk GPAI providers must conduct adversarial testing to identify and mitigate systemic risks
controls.title: Art. 55(1)(b) � Systemic risk GPAI adversarial testing
controls.description: Poisoning detection adversarial testing is a binding obligation for systemic risk models

controls.control_id: Ingestion integrity risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Data ingestion attack surfaces included in Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: High-risk AI resilient against adversarial input manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Secure ingestion interfaces and path traversal prevention are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Documented procedures for data ingestion and pipeline integrity
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Ingestion validation procedures and CVE patching documented in quality management

controls.control_id: Third-party tool data exchange risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: All tool integrations assessed in Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: Providers document obligations flowing to deployers; deployers verify
controls.title: Art. 25 � Value chain responsibilities
controls.description: Tool data exchange obligations explicitly distributed along the value chain

controls.control_id: GPAI providers document all third-party integrations
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Tool and plugin data exchange documented in GPAI technical documentation

controls.control_id: High-risk AI training data subject to governance � relevant, representative, privacy-preserving
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Data governance policy covering the full AI data lifecycle is an Art. 10 compliance requirement

controls.control_id: Documented quality management system including data handling procedures
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Data lifecycle procedures � classification, retention, deletion � documented in quality management

controls.control_id: GPAI providers maintain technical documentation including training data governance
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Full data governance documentation for GPAI training data is a binding Art. 53 obligation

controls.control_id: Mandatory risk management system for high-risk AI
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Absence of a documented risk management system is an Art. 9 violation

controls.control_id: Documented quality management system including post-market monitoring
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Absence of a quality management system is an Art. 17 violation; Post-market monitoring must detect and respond to sensitive disclosure incidents

controls.control_id: Graduated fines based on violation type
controls.title: Art. 72 � Fines
controls.description: Understanding fine exposure is the starting point for compliance prioritisation

controls.control_id: Data governance covers all input modalities � images, audio, video � not only text
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Multimodal input governance is an Art. 10 requirement for high-risk systems processing such inputs

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures covering all data channels including multimodal inputs
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: DLP and access controls on multimodal pipelines are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Technical documentation covers all input modalities processed by the model
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Multimodal data governance documented in GPAI technical documentation

controls.control_id: Training data must be subject to appropriate data governance � privacy measures must be effective
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Anonymisation effectiveness is an Art. 10 data governance requirement, not a self-certification

controls.control_id: GPAI training data governance documented � privacy measures included
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Synthetic data generation methodology and re-identification risk assessment documented for GPAI

controls.control_id: Data governance covering multi-tenant deployments � session isolation as a data handling requirement
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Session isolation controls are an Art. 10 data governance obligation for multi-user deployments

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures preventing cross-session data exposure
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Session isolation and tenant separation are Art. 15 technical requirements

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring detecting and responding to session bleed incidents
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Cross-session leakage detection in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: LLM gateway risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: All LLM-to-database interfaces mapped in Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: High-risk AI designed to allow effective human oversight � ability to pause, stop, and override
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: LLM-generated destructive database queries executing autonomously are an Art. 14 human oversight failure

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting against adversarial misuse
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Least-privilege execution, query allowlisting, and SQL injection prevention are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Documented procedures for LLM gateway security
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Security testing procedures and query log retention documented in quality management

controls.control_id: Vector store security risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: All vector store deployments assessed in Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: High-risk AI infrastructure protected against cybersecurity risks
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: RBAC, encryption, and CVE patching for vector stores are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering AI infrastructure components
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Vector store security monitoring in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: Data governance applies to all data in AI system scope � including telemetry and monitoring data
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Telemetry data governance � classification, access controls, retention � is an Art. 10 requirement

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring required � but the monitoring infrastructure must itself be secured
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Telemetry security is both an Art. 17 obligation and an Art. 10 data governance requirement

controls.control_id: Data governance applies to all data in AI system scope � including context window content
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Context window data governance � minimum content, classification tracking � is Art. 10 requirement

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity against adversarial exploitation of context window content
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Context minimisation and access controls are Art. 15 cybersecurity measures

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering context window exploitation incidents
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Context window security in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: Endpoint AI agent risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Browser and endpoint agent deployments assessed in Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: High-risk AI designed to allow human oversight � users must be able to stop and override
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Users must be able to pause and override endpoint AI agents � Art. 14 human oversight requirement

controls.control_id: Deployers ensure human oversight as instructed by provider
controls.title: Art. 29 � Deployer obligations
controls.description: Deployers responsible for ensuring endpoint AI agents operate within Art. 14 human oversight scope

controls.control_id: High-risk AI must remain accurate and available under adverse conditions
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Vector store redundancy, circuit breakers, and staleness detection are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering availability and resilience
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: AI pipeline availability monitoring in quality management and post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: Training data privacy measures required � protecting sensitive data used in training
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Differential privacy and inference attack resistance are Art. 10 data governance requirements

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting against attacks including inference attacks
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Output rate limiting, confidence score suppression, and embedding encryption are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: Training data governance includes human annotation workflows
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Labeller data access controls and data minimisation are Art. 10 requirements for high-risk training data

controls.control_id: Labelling vendors are part of the AI value chain � obligations distributed accordingly
controls.title: Art. 25 � Value chain responsibilities
controls.description: Labelling vendor security requirements and contractual controls are Art. 25 value chain obligations

controls.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting AI system integrity
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: API rate limiting, output perturbation, and extraction monitoring are Art. 15 requirements

controls.control_id: GPAI technical documentation includes model architecture and capability details
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Technical documentation must be protected � model extraction enables circumvention of documentation obligations

controls.control_id: Post-market monitoring covering security incidents
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Model extraction detection in post-market monitoring programme

controls.control_id: Training and retrieval data free from errors � source integrity required
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: RAG corpus integrity controls and source verification are Art. 10 requirements

controls.control_id: High-risk AI accurate and resilient against adversarial data manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Source trust scoring and ingestion validation are Art. 15 robustness requirements

controls.control_id: Systemic risk GPAI providers must assess and mitigate risks including disinformation
controls.title: Art. 55(1)(a) � Systemic risk GPAI
controls.description: Disinformation risk assessment and RAG integrity controls are binding Art. 55 obligations; Misinformation risk assessment and mitigation is a binding obligation for systemic risk models

controls.control_id: AI-generated content that could be mistaken as human must be disclosed
controls.title: Art. 50 � Transparency
controls.description: Disinformation risk amplified when users cannot distinguish AI-generated content � Art. 50 applies

controls.control_id: Providers of high-risk AI must implement a risk management system covering all reasonably foreseeable risks
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Prompt injection must be identified, analysed, and mitigated in the risk management system

controls.control_id: High-risk AI systems must be resilient to adversarial inputs and attempts to alter outputs or performance
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Robustness against prompt injection is a technical compliance requirement � not optional

controls.control_id: Providers of systemic risk GPAI models must conduct adversarial testing to identify and mitigate systemic risks
controls.title: Art. 55(1)(b) � Systemic risk GPAI
controls.description: Prompt injection adversarial testing is a binding obligation for systemic risk models

controls.control_id: Training data for high-risk AI must be relevant, representative, free of errors, and complete � must address privacy
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Privacy-preserving training data practices are a compliance requirement for high-risk LLMs

controls.control_id: Users must receive information about capabilities, limitations, and conditions of use
controls.title: Art. 13 � Transparency
controls.description: Disclosure of potential data disclosure risks is a transparency obligation

controls.control_id: Supply chain risks must be identified and mitigated in the risk management system
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Third-party component risks are in scope for Art. 9 risk management

controls.control_id: Quality management must cover supply chain controls
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Documented supply chain security procedures required

controls.control_id: Responsibilities distributed along the AI value chain between providers and deployers
controls.title: Art. 25 � Value chain responsibilities
controls.description: Providers must document what deployers inherit � deployers must verify

controls.control_id: GPAI providers must document training data governance including third-party sources
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(a) � GPAI documentation
controls.description: Third-party training data provenance is a GPAI documentation obligation

controls.control_id: Data poisoning must be identified as a foreseeable risk and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Poisoning attack scenarios required in Art. 9 risk assessment

controls.control_id: Training data must be subject to appropriate governance practices � relevant, representative, free of errors
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Data quality controls preventing poisoning are a compliance requirement

controls.control_id: Systemic risk GPAI providers must conduct adversarial testing to identify model-level risks
controls.title: Art. 55(1)(b) � Systemic risk GPAI adversarial testing
controls.description: Poisoning detection is in scope for Art. 55 adversarial testing

controls.control_id: Output handling risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Insecure output handling included in risk management system

controls.control_id: Accurate, robust outputs resilient to misuse
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Output validation and sanitisation are Art. 15 technical requirements

controls.control_id: Documented procedures covering output quality
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Post-market monitoring of output handling incidents required

controls.control_id: Autonomy-related risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Excessive agency scenarios required in risk management system

controls.control_id: High-risk AI systems designed to allow effective human oversight � ability to pause, stop, and override
controls.title: Art. 14 � Human oversight
controls.description: Human-in-the-loop requirements are a binding Art. 14 compliance obligation

controls.control_id: Deployers must ensure human oversight as instructed by provider
controls.title: Art. 29 � Deployer obligations
controls.description: Deployers cannot waive Art. 14 human oversight requirements

controls.control_id: Users must receive sufficient information about the AI system � but this does not require disclosing system prompts
controls.title: Art. 13 � Transparency
controls.description: Transparency obligation must be met without exposing security-sensitive system prompt content

controls.control_id: Configuration management documented
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: System prompt versions, access controls, and change procedures are quality management artefacts

controls.control_id: GPAI providers must publish summaries of training data and model capabilities
controls.title: Art. 53(1)(b) � GPAI transparency
controls.description: Published summaries must not inadvertently expose security-sensitive configuration

controls.control_id: Embedding and retrieval risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Vector store attack scenarios included in risk management system

controls.control_id: Data quality controls applied to all data in scope � including RAG corpora
controls.title: Art. 10 � Data and data governance
controls.description: Quality controls on embedding generation and vector store ingestion required

controls.control_id: Technical robustness against adversarial manipulation
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Embedding manipulation resistance is an Art. 15 technical requirement

controls.control_id: High-risk AI system information must include accuracy metrics and known limitations
controls.title: Art. 13 � Transparency
controls.description: Hallucination rates and accuracy limitations must be disclosed to deployers and users

controls.control_id: AI-generated content must be marked as such � chatbots must disclose AI nature
controls.title: Art. 50 � Transparency for certain AI systems
controls.description: Mandatory AI disclosure prevents users from treating hallucinated content as authoritative human output

controls.control_id: Availability risks identified and mitigated
controls.title: Art. 9 � Risk management
controls.description: Resource exhaustion and DoS scenarios included in risk management system

controls.control_id: High-risk AI must remain available under adversarial conditions
controls.title: Art. 15 � Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity
controls.description: Technical resilience against denial-of-service attacks is an Art. 15 requirement

controls.control_id: Operational continuity procedures documented
controls.title: Art. 17 � Quality management
controls.description: Post-market monitoring and incident response for availability failures required
